channel.title: Littoralicious
channel.description: Modern science and regional littoral cooking for yacht chefs.

item.title: Moist Carrot Cake: The Science-First Version
item.description: Toasted-walnut butter, macerated carrot, mascarpone-tempered cream cheese frosting. Three science-backed modifications that produce the moistest, most stable carrot cake you will ever serve on charter. Every step tested, every claim sourced.

item.title: Sticky Toffee Pudding: Dark Muscovado & Whisky Caramel
item.description: Dark muscovado, soda-bloomed dates, whisky caramel. Three modifications grounded in food chemistry that turn nursery dessert into a charter-grade course.

item.title: Apple Crumble: The Perfect Version
item.description: Two apple varieties. A 71°C pre-cook that activates pectin methylesterase. Brown-butter oat crumble. Flaky sea salt on top. Every failure mode fixed structurally.

item.title: Chicken Soup: The Perfect Version
item.description: Cold-start extraction from roasted bones. Chicken feet for gelatin. Meat poached separately, never boiled into cotton. Fish sauce and parmesan rind for invisible umami. Every step sourced.

item.title: Port Call Venice: Provisioning a City With No Roads
item.description: Port Call Venice: the three berths, Pesto Sea Group + the agent call, the Rialto Pescheria at dawn, Sant'Erasmo direct-supply, Mestre as the wholesale answer. Watch-outs for Biennale, Redentore, acqua alta, Ferragosto. Built from working yacht-chef intel.

item.title: Garlic: The Immortal Bulb
item.description: 7,000 years of cultivation. Found in Tutankhamun's tomb. Fed to Olympic athletes. Feared by vampires. Five forms in your galley. The complete guide to the most important ingredient in human history.

item.title: Mushrooms: The New Centre of the Plate
item.description: Shiitakes replace steak. King oyster sears like escalope. A good mushroom has the umami of aged meat and the texture of a noble product. The complete guide to the ingredient rewriting the centre of the plate.

item.title: Tea as a Cooking Ingredient
item.description: Not tea as a beverage. Tea as a cooking ingredient. Hojicha in sauces, lapsang souchong in jus, matcha in marinades. The aromatic complexity that classical fonds cannot reproduce.

item.title: Banana Bread: The Science-First Version
item.description: Brown butter, roasted bananas, white miso. Three science-backed modifications that transform banana bread. Every step tested, every claim sourced.

item.title: Port Call: Provisioning Guides Built by the Chefs Who Actually Dock There
item.description: Port Call is the provisioning playbook series: three berths, real suppliers with phone + address, inline cautions, watch-outs by season. Venice + Miami live. St. Barth, Antibes, Palma incoming.

item.title: FlavorDB: The Cheat Code for Flavor Pairing
item.description: FlavorDB maps 25,595 flavor molecules across 936 ingredients. Here's how to use it to build dishes based on shared volatile compounds — not tradition, not intuition, but chemistry.

item.title: Claude Code in the Galley: The Power Tool
item.description: Claude Code turns your galley into a system — PDF menu cards, guest intelligence from WhatsApp exports, automated handover notes, and a shared brain between rotational chefs.

item.title: The Truth About Paying Tax as Yacht Crew
item.description: The truth about paying tax as yacht crew. What Europeans, Americans, South Africans, and Australians actually owe — and what most people get dangerously wrong.

item.title: How to Become a Yacht Chef: The Real Path Nobody Advertises
item.description: The real path to becoming a yacht chef. Certifications, day work, crew agencies, what to cook at a trial, and what nobody tells you before your first season.

item.title: Technology in the Galley: Your AI Sous Chef
item.description: AI is the most powerful galley tool since the immersion circulator. Here's how to use it for menus, provisioning, guest preferences, and recipe development — with a ready-to-use menu agent you can copy into any AI system.

item.title: WhatsApp as Your Trip Intelligence System
item.description: Two WhatsApp groups. One chat export. Every guest preference, every broken gasket, every handover note — captured automatically, timestamped, and ready to process with AI. The complete trip-closing system for yacht chefs.

item.title: Provisioning Miami & Fort Lauderdale: The Complete Playbook
item.description: The complete provisioning playbook for Miami and Fort Lauderdale. Three berths, wholesale infrastructure, off-the-boat fish, the Hialeah Asian stock-up, season-aware cautions. Built from working yacht-chef intel.

item.title: The Fat Index: Every Cooking Fat Ranked by Science
item.description: 17 cooking fats ranked by nutrient density, cooking versatility, omega profile, and evidence base. The definitive science-backed reference for your galley.

item.title: The Salt Index: Every Culinary Salt Ranked by Science
item.description: Every culinary salt compared by mineral content, crystal structure, dissolution rate, and practical application. The definitive science-backed salt reference.

item.title: The Acid Index: Every Culinary Acid Ranked by Science
item.description: Every culinary acid ranked by pH, flavour profile, heat stability, and practical application. Citrus, vinegars, fermented acids, and mineral acids — the definitive reference.

item.title: Cooked: How Fire Made Us Human and Why the Person Holding the Pan Still Matters
item.description: Without cooked food, no human brains, no culture, no civilisation. The science of how fire made us who we are — and why the person holding the pan still matters.

item.title: Sea Urchin: Seasonal Guide, Compound Profile, and Sourcing
item.description: Sea urchin compound profile, seasonal peak data, and sourcing guidelines for yacht chefs. Paracentrotus lividus at pre-spawning maximum.

item.title: Sea Urchin Beurre Blanc: Roe as Natural Emulsifier
item.description: Use raw sea urchin roe to stabilise a beurre blanc without cream or starch. The roe's phospholipids do the work while its glutamic acid amplifies umami.

item.title: Umami in Western Cuisine: Fermentation as the Universal Glutamate Engine
item.description: Fermentation is the universal mechanism for generating free glutamate. Western food traditions have been doing it for centuries. The data, the galley implications, and the limitations.

item.title: Spaghetti ai Ricci di Mare: Mondello, Sicily
item.description: Spaghetti ai ricci di mare from Mondello, Sicily. Four ingredients, one coastline, no shortcuts. The dish exists because sea urchins were free.

item.title: Myojaku Earns Three Stars and STCW Anti-Harassment Training Goes Mandatory
item.description: Myojaku earns three Michelin stars by stripping umami back to the ingredient itself. Plus: STCW anti-harassment training goes mandatory for all flag states.

item.title: The Silver Lining Protocol: A Science-Backed Survival Guide for Galley Sanity
item.description: 82% of superyacht crew report low mood or anxiety onboard. The psychology of submarines, Antarctic stations, and NASA missions offers a tested playbook.

item.title: The Pharmacy in Your Spice Rack: What Science Actually Knows About Medicinal Plants in Food
item.description: Cacao, turmeric, saffron, cinnamon — the plants that shaped civilisations and still protect us. The science behind why ancient ingredients work.
